prompt:
A Research Resource & Grant Administration system with main features including secure submissions, journal catalog governance, resource booking, policy-driven funding workflows, explainable recommendations, and offline-capable payment settlement. The Svelte-based web interface serves four primary roles: Researchers submit subsidy or project applications, upload required materials, and track progress; Reviewers and Approvers evaluate eligibility and sign off in multi-level steps; Resource Managers maintain bookable assets such as study rooms, equipment, or consultation slots; and Finance Clerks reconcile fees and refunds. Applicants start by selecting a funding policy, viewing required-document templates, and uploading documents, images, archives, or external links with in-browser preview for PDFs and images; each resubmission creates a new version with one-click rollback, up to 20 versions per file. Deadlines are shown in local time with clear late rules, such as a 24-hour grace window marked "Late," after which submissions are blocked unless an Administrator grants a one-time extension. Resource booking presents availability by business hours (default 8:00 AM–6:00 PM, Monday–Friday), highlights conflicts in real time, and displays capacity (for example, a room capped at 6 occupants) with visible maintenance blackouts. Personalized recommendations surface relevant journals, funding programs, and bookable resources; users can like, mark "not interested," or block items, edit preferences, and view plain-language "Why this is recommended" explanations such as "matches your selected research areas and recent bookings." For paid services (rent, deposit, service fee), the UI supports creating an invoice, recording a WeChat Pay reference, and initiating full or partial refunds, while clearly flagging unsettled or exception states for follow-up.

The backend uses Fastify to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Svelte frontend, with PostgreSQL as the local system of record for users, roles, applications, approvals, journals, resources, recommendations, and financial ledgers. Authentication is strictly offline with username and password, enforcing at least 10 characters with complexity rules, salted hashing, and a 15-minute lockout after 5 failed attempts; sensitive fields (for example, bank routing numbers entered for refunds) are encrypted at rest and masked in logs. Upload security is enforced server-side through size and format validation (default 200 MB per file), MIME sniffing, archive expansion limits to prevent zip bombs, executable-file blocking, and lightweight sensitive-pattern detection (for example, SSN-like sequences) that triggers a warning or administrative hold; every download can apply a watermark containing username and timestamp and is governed by role-based permissions. Workflow validation automatically checks eligibility criteria, duplicate applications within the same policy period, and funding caps such as $5,000.00 per applicant per fiscal year, then routes approvals through up to three levels with required comments and immutable audit trails. Journal master data supports full CRUD with version history, attachments (contracts, quotes, sample issues), and admin-defined custom fields without schema-breaking changes. Payment "integration" remains offline by storing WeChat Pay transaction identifiers and supporting CSV import of settlement exports for reconciliation; discrepancies such as "paid but not matched to an invoice" are placed into an exception queue until resolved, and all ledger postings record who performed the action and when. stack: Frontend stack

modern_frontend_framework

Backend stack

Node.js

Database

PostgreSQL
